notes: Object-position 'dia' → English 'them' (singular-they object form). 'ketemu' is the colloquial 'meet/run into'. The NMT typically handles position transformation (dia → them in English object slot) without needing the rule to inject — the rule's primary value here is the marker tag preventing the NMT from picking he/she. VERIFY WITH GIRLFRIEND: 'them' for a single person can read awkward in some registers ('I ran into him/her yesterday' is more colloquial when gender is known). This fixture stays gender-neutral as the safe default.
